title: Doubles Coach Tan Kim Her Pairs Up Hariharan-Arjun After Satwik-Chirag
indian_angle: This story highlights the strategic role of Indian doubles coach Tan Kim Her in developing new Indian talent like Hariharan and Arjun, following his success with Satwik-Chirag.
summary: Doubles coach Tan Kim Her, known for his work with Satwik-Chirag, has now paired up Hariharan and Arjun, aiming to develop another strong Indian men's doubles combination.

title: PV Sindhu Needs More Self-Belief To Overcome An Se-young
indian_angle: This piece analyzes what PV Sindhu needs to improve to consistently beat top opponents like An Se-young, a crucial aspect for Indian fans hoping for her continued success.
summary: An analysis suggests that PV Sindhu's primary challenge against world No. 1 An Se-young is a lack of self-belief, which she needs to cultivate to turn the tide in their matchups.

title: Ayush Shetty Challenges World No 15 Weng Hong Yang
indian_angle: Young Indian shuttler Ayush Shetty's strong performance against a higher-ranked Chinese opponent signals his potential and gives Indian fans a new talent to follow.
summary: Emerging Indian shuttler Ayush Shetty put up a strong fight against Chinese World No. 15 Weng Hong Yang, indicating his growing prowess on the international circuit.

title: Sindhu, Arjun-Hariharan Advance To Indonesia Open Round 2
indian_angle: This report details the progress of key Indian players PV Sindhu and the new men's doubles pair Arjun and Hariharan at a major international tournament.
summary: PV Sindhu and the men's doubles pair of Arjun and Hariharan successfully navigated their opening matches to reach the second round of the Indonesia Open.

title: BAI Invites Expression Of Interest For BWF World Championships 2026
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) inviting Expressions of Interest to host the BWF World Championships 2026 is a significant step towards bringing a prestigious global event to India.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has issued an Expression of Interest for potential hosts of the BWF World Championships 2026, signaling India's ambition to stage the major event.

title: Satwik-Chirag Crowned Singapore Open Champions After Thrilling Final
indian_angle: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty's victory at the Singapore Open is a major triumph for Indian badminton on the international stage.
summary: India's top men's doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, secured the Singapore Open title after a series of furious rallies and a dominant performance in the final.
